sample.Inventory: Inventory Analysis covers the discovery and inventory side of Site24x7 -- monitor inventory and import, monitor dependency mapping, network discovery and polling configuration, location/metric/server profiles, credential storage, alert rules, and license usage. Note: every response body captured in the source data for this sheet was sanitized during an earlier merge step (response bodies show only a removal note rather than real content); endpoint paths, methods, and status codes are real, but response content should be treated as not captured for this sheet specifically.

sample.__EMPTY_3: Lists all configured AI/Zia workflow definitions.

sample.User & Alert Management: User & Alert Management covers user accounts, roles and permissions, user groups, on-call schedules, alert categories, attribute groups/details used for alert filtering, and the change tracker that logs configuration changes across the account.

sample.__EMPTY_3: Lists all configured AI/Zia workflow definitions.

sample.Configuration Profiles: Configuration Profiles consolidates the 16 underlying configuration sections explored earlier in this project: Location Profile, Threshold and Availability, Notification Profile, Business Hours, Email Templates, OAuth Providers, Web Tokens, APM Agent Configuration, APM Business Transaction Configuration, APM Business Transaction Rules, Credential Profile, Metric Profile, Mobile Application Profile, Anomaly Settings, Auto Upgrade Profile, and Custom Report. Each row's Sub-Feature column names which of these 16 sections it belongs to. Rows tagged 'General App Bootstrap' or 'Unrelated' are app-wide startup calls or incidental traffic not specific to any one of the 16 sections.

sample.Automations: Automations covers Site24x7's IT Automation feature -- creating, listing, scheduling, and viewing logs for automated remediation actions -- alongside AI/Zia workflow definitions, APM Insight checks, dashboards, outage and alarm reporting, and third-party integrations (Cliq, Circuits, Qntrl) that automations can trigger into.

sample.__EMPTY_3: Lists all configured AI/Zia workflow definitions.

sample.Zia Settings: Zia Settings covers Site24x7's AI layer (branded Zia): AI-generated recommendations, AI task management, AI workflow CRUD operations, AI-curated monitor collections, and the underlying bot/AI model configuration.

sample.__EMPTY_3: Lists AI data collections available for Zia workflow processing.

sample.Server Monitor: Server Monitor covers server-specific monitoring configuration: server templates and their associated monitors, resource profiles and setting profiles (CRUD and their monitor associations), threshold profiles, upgrade profiles for monitoring agents, APM auto-profiler rules, and server-specific features and notification profiles.

sample.__EMPTY_3: Lists AI data collections available for Zia workflow processing.

sample.Plugin Monitor: Plugin Monitor lets Site24x7 collect custom metrics from scripts (plugins) running on a server, rather than relying only on built-in monitor types. It has two sub-features: 'Settings' is a single account-wide toggle controlling whether plugins are auto-discovered and added when found on a server. 'Plugin Template Config' is a list of registered plugin templates (e.g. iNodeMon.py, mysql_monitoring.py, Redis.py), each showing a version number and how many distinct metrics (attributes) that template reports. Clicking a template row does not open any further detail in the UI, so this list view is the complete picture for that sub-feature.

sample.__EMPTY_3: Fetches data for: Plugin Template Config (endpoint: /app/api/plugin/template/275039000043639001)
sample.__EMPTY_5: {"error_code":1100,"message":"You are not authorized to perform this operation."}

sample.Operations: Operations groups five distinct features used for day-to-day monitoring operations rather than initial setup. 'Scheduled Maintenance' lets you define planned downtime windows (once/daily/weekly/monthly/yearly) for specific monitors so expected work doesn't trigger false alerts. 'Status Pages' is StatusIQ, a separate customer-facing app for publishing public incident/status pages. 'Schedule IT Automation' runs an automated remediation script on a recurring schedule, independent of any maintenance window. 'Log Report' pulls a historical event log for a single monitor over a chosen date range (Site24x7's internal API name for this is 'log_reports'). 'Alert Logs' is an account-wide, filterable audit log of every alert ever sent across all monitors and channels (Site24x7's internal API name for this is 'applog', short for Application Log). A number of rows below are tagged as supporting lookups or dashboard-widget rendering -- these are real API calls but feed multiple sub-features or general UI widgets rather than belonging to one specific feature.

sample.My Account: My Account configures the logged-in user's environment and the organization's account-wide settings -- this is administrative configuration, not monitoring configuration. 'Personal Settings' covers the user's own profile and status message; most identity actions (password, email, MFA) route to a separate Zoho Accounts service rather than Site24x7's own API. 'Web Client Notification Settings' controls in-app UI preferences such as the snackbar/toast notification language and behavior. 'Account Settings' covers org-wide settings such as industry, country, account-level language, time zone and hour format for reports, and the default landing page shown after login.

sample.Report Settings: Report Settings has three sub-features. 'Scheduled Reports' lets you set up a report to be generated and delivered automatically on a schedule; the UI actually exposes two materially different creation forms depending on report type (Custom Dashboard-based reports vs. monitor-based reports like Availability Summary Report), so expect different payload shapes depending on which type of report is being created. 'Customize Report' is a single, account-wide set of branding and formatting defaults (logo, colors, company name, decimal precision, percentiles) applied to all generated reports. 'SLA Settings' defines measurable uptime/performance commitments (Availability SLA, Composite SLA, Response Time SLA) tracked over time, separate from simple alerting. Several rows below are tagged as supporting lookups -- these populate dropdown choices (business hours, monitor groups, tags, plugins) inside the Scheduled Reports forms rather than being a feature in their own right.

sample.__EMPTY_5: {"error_code":1010,"message":"Access Denied. Sign Up for Site24x7 to access this feature."}

sample.Share: Share covers three features for exposing monitoring data outside of Site24x7's own interface. 'Uptime Buttons' generates a small embeddable badge showing a monitor's uptime percentage, meant to be placed on an external website. 'Operations Dashboard' creates a shareable/embeddable multi-monitor dashboard (Site24x7's internal API name for this is 'dashboard_views'), intended for things like a NOC wall display; viewing an existing one shows a permalink and embed code separate from the create form. 'Public Reports' publishes a single specific report (Summary, Availability, Performance, Top N, etc.) as a public link/embed without granting the viewer any login access, and likewise has a separate permalink/embed view distinct from the publish form. Several rows below are tagged as supporting lookups -- these populate dropdown choices used by more than one of the three features above.

sample.Milestones: The Milestones module allows users to create timeline markers associated with monitoring events. These markers help correlate infrastructure, application, or operational changes with monitoring data and incidents.

sample.__EMPTY_3: Manages default marker display and milestone marker behavior. Attempts to update the default milestone marker configuration (e.g. marker name, color, or display settings). Returns 400 in demo environment.

sample.Third-Party Integrations: Third-Party Integrations allows Site24x7 to connect with external tools across incident management, collaboration, workflow automation, analytics, AI, application management, and security categories. Each integration type has a dedicated API endpoint for configuration, creation, update, and deletion.

sample.__EMPTY_3: Creates or updates a Webhook integration configuration that sends monitor alerts to external HTTP endpoints. Called multiple times in session for different webhook configurations.

sample.Tags: The Tags module allows users to organize and classify monitors using custom labels. Tags help simplify filtering, reporting, grouping, and monitor management across the Site24x7 platform.

sample.__EMPTY_3: Retrieves the full list of configured tags in the account, including tag name, color, and type.

sample.Downloads: The Downloads module provides monitoring agents, installers, and supporting resources required to integrate monitors into the Site24x7 platform. Users can download APM agents (Java, .NET, Ruby, PHP, Python, Node.js), device agents, and mobile apps, with each download triggering an activity counter.

sample.__EMPTY_3: Records the Java APM agent download event and initiates the agent download workflow. Returns 401 in demo environment.
